rulings.Case Title: BRUCE VANDERHOFF et al VS. PNEUMO ABEX LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 29, 2016, line 2. DEFENDANT AEROJET-GENERAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Aerojet-General Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Further, defendant's moving papers never argued that plaintiff's identification of them as Aerojet employees is inadmissible. However, having considered the objection, plaintiff's identification of Aerojet employees is admissible. Plaintiff testified that Aerojet employees exposed him to pipe insulation that contained asbestos, and defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not have knowledge that the insulation at issue contained asbestos. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826 for this issue. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it is not liable for injuries sustained by plaintiff while on defendant's premises. Defendant failed to demonstrate that the premises liability authority it relies on bars plaintiff's claims against it. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from sustaining its initial burden because it demonstrates that defendant's alleged liability is based on the affirmative conduct of defendant's employees, not on the theories discussed in defendant's authority. That is, even if defendant is correct that plaintiff cannot demonstrate that defendant failed to warn of a concealed, pre-existing hazardous condition on the premises or negligently exercised control over plaintiff's work, defendant still would not demonstrate that plaintiff cannot successfully assert his Negligence II cause of action against it. (See Biles v. Exxon Mobil Corp. (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 1315.) Finally, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that any liability for asbestos exposure on the Aerojet premises rests with plaintiff's employer pursuant to SeaBright Ins. Co. v. US Airways, Inc. (2011) 52 Cal.4th 590. Defendant's objections to excerpts of plaintiff's deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 29, 2016, line 1. INTERVENORS MARYLAND CASUALTY COMPANY, FIREMAN'S FUND INSUARANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Intervenors Maryland Casualty Company's and Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's, on behalf of Marconi Plastering, motion for summary judgment is denied. Intervenors failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Marconi. Intervenors' failure to demonstrate via Mr. Granados' deposition testimony that he has no such knowledge and plaintiffs' responses to intervenors' special interrogatories preclude intervenors from sustaining their burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Specifically, while intervenors addressed the UCSF site, they failed to demonstrate that the UCSF site was the only time Mr. Granados worked in the presence of Marconi employees during his career. Plaintiffs' argument that the stipulation for a shortened notice period has been withdrawn by their lawyer is rejected by the Court. No enforceable stipulation has been shown and even if the email correspondence is considered an enforceable stipulation, it contained no language demonstrating the intent of the parties as to whether the failure to comply with the terms of the agreement would have any consequences or penalties. Therefore intervenors' motion is not denied on the basis that they failed to send plaintiffs a Word version of its Separate Statement at the time it served its moving papers. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
